page: Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It features 8 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, with 8 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 320 GB SSD. The hypervisor layer is managed via QEMU.

In benchmark evaluations, the cpx42 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% in stress-ng single-core tests and the top 25% in Geekbench single-core tests. Multi-core performance for both stress-ng and Geekbench falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth for large-block read and write operations is strong, ranking in the top 25%. Large-block uncached reads reach 255.54 GB/sec, while writes reach 168.09 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI), which assist in workloads like lightweight LLM text generation where it achieves strong performance on small models.

From a resource density perspective, the cpx42 offers a compute-leaning profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high single-thread performance without massive memory footprints. However, because the CPU allocation is shared, users may experience performance variability under sustained multi-core loads compared to dedicated thread instances. This server is suited for web serving, software compilation, database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks that benefit from AVX-512 vector extensions.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance featuring 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a bundled 320 GB local SSD. Operating on the QEMU hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% in stress-ng benchmarks, alongside strong memory bandwidth for large-block operations. While multi-core performance remains average due to its shared CPU allocation, the processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. This configuration represents a cost-efficient, compute-leaning option for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference that prioritize single-thread speed over dedicated multi-core resources.

tagline: An AMD EPYC Genoa virtual server featuring high single-core performance, a compute-leaning memory ratio, and bundled SSD storage.
